jokes.text: Leslie insisting on interviewing for her own job with Ron, who repeatedly tells her to just take it
jokes.setup: Leslie wants to interview for deputy director position
jokes.punchline: It's already her job and Ron was her boss
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - her neurotic need for process even when it's absurd. Ron's exasperation is the straight-man perfect foil.

jokes.text: I also walked you down the aisle at your wedding
jokes.setup: Ron listing reasons why Leslie doesn't need to interview
jokes.punchline: The increasingly personal nature of their relationship
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that deepens their relationship dynamic. Shows Ron's softer side through gruff delivery.

jokes.text: Give it all back to the taxpayers
jokes.setup: Leslie gives detailed answer about park department goals
jokes.punchline: Ron's libertarian philosophy making him terrible at his government job
jokes.explanation: Pure Ron Swanson - his anti-government stance while being a government employee is consistently funny and well-integrated.

jokes.text: Spirit dogs chart showing everyone's assigned dog breeds
jokes.setup: First morning meeting in old conference room
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous chart categorizing colleagues as dog breeds
jokes.explanation: Funny visual concept that establishes the office's quirky dynamics in Leslie's absence.

jokes.text: You guys think I'm a border collie? So obviously a cocker spaniel
jokes.setup: Leslie sees her spirit dog assignment
jokes.punchline: Her immediate disagreement with the assessment
jokes.explanation: Leslie's need to control/correct everything, even silly office games. Mildly amusing character moment.

jokes.text: Classic border collie. Has to control everything
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to redirect focus from spirit dogs to work
jokes.punchline: Tom proving the dog assignment was accurate
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that proves the spirit dog thing was actually insightful. Great character observation.

jokes.text: Can I get a what-what? What? What? Thank you
jokes.setup: Tom asking for enthusiasm about his lemonade idea
jokes.punchline: Confused responses that he interprets as success
jokes.explanation: Tom's obliviousness to social cues creates mild cringe comedy. Not particularly clever but fits character.

jokes.text: Champagne app that pops whenever I say something awesome
jokes.setup: Tom explaining his business success
jokes.punchline: He downloaded an app to celebrate himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom vanity - paying for digital validation. Good character integration with modern tech absurdity.

jokes.text: I know. And it was only 9 bucks
jokes.setup: Leslie says 'Tom, that's great' about the lemonade
jokes.punchline: Tom thinks she's praising the app purchase
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection showing Tom's priorities. Quick and efficient character comedy.

jokes.text: Larry's endless romantic rambling about love making the world okay
jokes.setup: Simple question about wedding plans
jokes.punchline: Larry launches into overwrought romantic speech
jokes.explanation: Larry's tendency to overshare and ramble. The length itself becomes the joke but it's not economical.

jokes.text: That literally went on forever. I thought you were never gonna stop talking
jokes.setup: Larry's long romantic speech
jokes.punchline: Chris acknowledging what audience was thinking
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment that acknowledges the comedy while setting up the proposal. Efficient and self-aware.

jokes.text: Ann Perkins, will you marry me?
jokes.setup: Chris calling out Larry's rambling
jokes.punchline: Immediately launches into romantic proposal
jokes.explanation: Great comedic timing - criticizing romantic speech then immediately being romantic. Good misdirection structure.

jokes.text: Cracker dust
jokes.setup: Romantic proposal moment
jokes.punchline: Ann choking on crackers during proposal
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that undercuts romantic moment. Simple slapstick but effective in context.

jokes.text: I'm a bit of a germaphobe
jokes.setup: Ann asking for water while choking
jokes.punchline: Chris won't share water during medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris character moment - health obsession overriding empathy even during proposals.

jokes.text: Oh, no, no, totally understand.
jokes.setup: Ann asks for water while choking, Chris refuses due to germaphobia
jokes.punchline: Ann politely accepts being denied help while choking
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Ann's politeness even in crisis. Character-driven humor.

jokes.text: No part of this is how I pictured it
jokes.setup: Chaotic choking proposal scene
jokes.punchline: Ann's dry assessment of romantic moment
jokes.explanation: Good understated reaction to absurd situation. Ann's practical nature cuts through romantic chaos.

jokes.text: What the hell is wrong with you? Are you in a cult? Can I join?
jokes.setup: Ben acting unusually friendly
jokes.punchline: April's blunt assessment and immediate interest
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - immediate suspicion followed by dark curiosity. Great character voice and quotable line.

jokes.text: Let's sacrifice Larry
jokes.setup: Talk about cult joining
jokes.punchline: April immediately suggests sacrificing Larry
jokes.explanation: Dark April humor with Larry as target. Perfectly in character and efficiently delivered.

jokes.text: Everyone is the best. We're cutting vacation time
jokes.setup: Ben trying to be nice and tough simultaneously
jokes.punchline: Contradictory messages delivered together
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of Ben's conflicted management style. Clear contradiction creates comedy.

jokes.text: I can't shower in five minutes
jokes.setup: Ben's five-minute bathroom break rule
jokes.punchline: Donna revealing she showers at work
jokes.explanation: Good Donna character moment - casual admission of treating workplace as personal spa.

jokes.text: that little dude's wound so tight, he's gonna pop.
jokes.setup: April observing Ben's contradictory behavior
jokes.punchline: Predicts his breakdown with casual confidence
jokes.explanation: Good April observation with typical detached analysis. Sets up Ben's later breakdown.

jokes.text: Everyone is a blue flyer except Ron
jokes.setup: New kangaroo classification system
jokes.punchline: Lazy categorization that defeats the purpose
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom's half-hearted effort at organization. Mildly amusing but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Step one: Chill out a bit. Step two: Get up there and rip it
jokes.setup: Leslie's complex project system
jokes.punchline: Tom's overly simple two-step plan
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Leslie's over-preparation and Tom's casual approach. Efficient character comedy.

jokes.text: Indiana blue ribbon panel on irrigation and drainage
jokes.setup: Leslie citing prestigious source
jokes.punchline: Incredibly niche bureaucratic panel
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie - treating obscure bureaucratic validation as meaningful praise. Great character specificity.

jokes.text: Big kangaroo.
jokes.setup: Leslie says she's the big dog
jokes.punchline: Tom corrects to kangaroo terminology
jokes.explanation: Weak callback that doesn't add much humor. Feels like filler rather than earned comedy.

jokes.text: When did they put a lamp here?
jokes.setup: Leslie asserting dominance over her territory
jokes.punchline: Immediately walks into furniture
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy undercutting character's confident moment. Simple but effective timing.

jokes.text: Are you trying to bribe us?
jokes.setup: Ben trying to share cheese with customs agents
jokes.punchline: Innocent gesture interpreted as bribery
jokes.explanation: Ben's naive attempt to be friendly making situation worse. Good escalation of minor problem.

jokes.text: This isn't a shotgun wedding, although, yes, she is pregnant, and, yes, we did just decide to get married today
jokes.setup: Jewelry store employee assuming shotgun wedding
jokes.punchline: Chris denying it while listing all evidence supporting it
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic structure - denying something while proving it true. Great Chris character obliviousness.

jokes.text: That's just for the rehearsal dinner
jokes.setup: Couple describing elaborate wedding planning
jokes.punchline: All that complexity is just for practice dinner
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke about wedding industry excess. Shows contrast with Chris and Ann's casual approach.

jokes.text: I placed a single strand of hair along the edge, and that secret strand is unbroken
jokes.setup: Leslie checking if Tom read her binder
jokes.punchline: Elaborate spy technique for tracking reading
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie paranoia and over-preparation. Creative and specific to her character's control issues.

jokes.text: Whose hair is that? It's brown
jokes.setup: Leslie's hair trap revelation
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately noticing hair color discrepancy
jokes.explanation: Tom's attention to beauty/style details even in weird situations. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: It's the most effective hair for binder security. You know that, Ron
jokes.setup: Tom questioning the hair trap system
jokes.punchline: Leslie treating it as common knowledge with elk hair expertise
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Leslie has researched optimal hair types for surveillance. Perfectly absurd and specific.

jokes.text: You're pranked. Check this out. I took a picture
jokes.setup: Serious customs investigation
jokes.punchline: Elaborate prank reveal with photo documentation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure but the prank feels somewhat disconnected from main characters.

jokes.text: Wow. Thi--you got me. Good one, makin' me think I was gonna like die in federal prison... Ah, that's super funny, you scamps.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to be a good sport about the prank
jokes.punchline: Awkward, forced appreciation while clearly traumatized
jokes.explanation: Good cringe showing Ben's struggle to appear cool while clearly shaken. Character-appropriate awkwardness.

jokes.text: Screw bisque
jokes.setup: Stu wanting to retire and try new soup styles
jokes.punchline: Leslie's passionate anti-bisque stance
jokes.explanation: Perfectly quotable Leslie intensity applied to something trivial. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Stew flutes toast
jokes.setup: Celebrating Stu staying with stew business
jokes.punchline: Toasting with tiny stew portions in fancy glassware
jokes.explanation: Funny visual concept - treating stew like champagne. Good absurdist touch.

jokes.text: You let me pee with the door open so I could watch the end of Grey's Anatomy
jokes.setup: Ann listing examples of their unconventional relationship
jokes.punchline: Oversharing intimate bathroom/TV arrangement
jokes.explanation: Good oversharing moment that shows their comfortable intimacy. Specific and relatable detail.

jokes.text: Just take it and get outta here
jokes.setup: Multiple ring purchase/return cycles
jokes.punchline: Jewelry store owner giving up on profit
jokes.explanation: Good escalation payoff to the indecision storyline. Salesperson's exasperation is relatable.

jokes.text: Listen. I know that you're the governor or whatever,
jokes.setup: Larry giving advice to Ben
jokes.punchline: Vague understanding of Ben's actual important position
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry being sort of right but not quite. Good character consistency and economy.

jokes.text: I'm just government junior
jokes.setup: Andy giving Ben advice about leadership
jokes.punchline: Andy's made-up rank for himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy - creating his own incorrect terminology while being surprisingly wise.

jokes.text: I feel like you're blowing it. If you want people to like you, you have to play along.
jokes.setup: Ben struggling with boss/friend balance
jokes.punchline: Larry giving social advice to anyone
jokes.explanation: Good irony of socially awkward Larry giving social advice. Character reversal works well.

jokes.text: I once did the best prank of all time. I dumped all of my boss's action figures into a fish tank. Yeah, that was me. And I didn't really like it
jokes.setup: Andy bragging about his pranking skills
jokes.punchline: He's describing pranking Ben and Ben remembers it negatively
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to earlier seasons with perfect dramatic irony. Andy's obliviousness to Ben's reaction is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Second of all, who uses mousse still?
jokes.setup: Hair product argument between Tom and Leslie
jokes.punchline: Tom's horror at outdated styling products
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom vanity priority - even during arguments he's focused on grooming standards.

jokes.text: No, I use the juicy space thing--whatever you do.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to prove she's current with hair products
jokes.punchline: Clearly has no idea what she's talking about
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment showing her obliviousness to fashion/style trends despite trying to keep up.

jokes.text: I don't know! Me either! Maybe let's not? Yeah, I think maybe not
jokes.setup: Second proposal attempt
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire uncertainty and decision reversal
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their indecision pattern. Shows their neurotic over-thinking nature.

jokes.text: Larry still can't see purple
jokes.setup: Discussing Tom's past presentation failures
jokes.punchline: Absurd lasting consequence from laser show mishap
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of casual reference to permanent disability. Perfectly absurd consequence.

jokes.text: He picked up the laser and stared right into it
jokes.setup: Defending the laser incident
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredibly stupid behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry stupidity explanation. Makes the injury both Tom's and Larry's fault.

jokes.text: I hired these guys in ski masks to kidnap us, and I was gonna pretend to be a hero
jokes.setup: Ben trying to execute prank
jokes.punchline: Revealing elaborate fake kidnapping plot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben over-thinking - turning simple prank into elaborate psychological thriller. Great character specificity.

jokes.text: They were gonna shoot me and drive the van into the river, and that's when the divers would go in
jokes.setup: Already absurd prank plan
jokes.punchline: Even more elaborate fake death and rescue scenario
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Ben's complete disconnect from normal social behavior. Perfectly absurd over-planning.

jokes.text: What the fuck?
jokes.setup: Ben's insane prank revelation
jokes.punchline: April's rare moment of genuine shock
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat - when April is shocked, you know it's truly insane. Great economy and character moment.

jokes.text: Best prank ever.
jokes.setup: Ben's prank goes wrong with fake blood everywhere
jokes.punchline: April genuinely impressed by the disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect April appreciation for chaos and failure. Character-appropriate reaction to disaster.

jokes.text: Bent over a boiling cauldron of steaming, bubbling meat
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to make Stu's job sound appealing
jokes.punchline: Describing it like medieval torture
jokes.explanation: Leslie's complete misunderstanding of persuasion. Makes the job sound horrible while trying to promote it.

jokes.text: Pungent beef smoothies until we bury him under that shed
jokes.setup: Leslie's increasingly negative sales pitch
jokes.punchline: Morbid conclusion to lifetime stew service
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation to dark conclusion. Leslie's enthusiasm leading to accidentally morbid imagery.

jokes.text: Hey. Rip it
jokes.setup: Formal presentation setting after Leslie's disaster
jokes.punchline: Tom's casual callback to his simple presentation philosophy
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with great timing contrast to Leslie's failure. Excellent economy and confidence.

jokes.text: R-e-s-p-e-g-c
jokes.setup: Discussion about respect
jokes.punchline: April deliberately misspelling Aretha Franklin song
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - deliberately getting cultural reference wrong to be annoying. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: According to Urethra Franklin
jokes.setup: Correcting April's spelling
jokes.punchline: April's deliberate mispronunciation of Aretha Franklin
jokes.explanation: Brilliant April character moment - combining crude humor with cultural reference. Perfect delivery vehicle.

jokes.text: According to Urethra Franklin, that's exactly how it's spelled.
jokes.setup: Ben misspells respect in a song reference
jokes.punchline: April's deadpan mispronunciation of Aretha Franklin
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that fits April's tendency to mangle references on purpose. Quotable delivery.

jokes.text: Enough with the mousse, for real
jokes.setup: Ben giving Andy hair product as gift
jokes.punchline: Continuing the mousse obsession from earlier
jokes.explanation: Minor callback that ties up hair product thread. Functional but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: No. We do not
jokes.setup: Leslie asking if department needs her anymore
jokes.punchline: April's blunt immediate response
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan delivery with emotional honesty. Great economy and character truth.

jokes.text: It's why I hired them. Then you betrayed me and turned them into efficient and caring government employees
jokes.setup: Ron explaining why he hired April and Tom for apathy
jokes.punchline: Treating good employee development as betrayal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron perspective - viewing competence as corruption of his anti-government mission. Excellent character consistency.

jokes.text: Was that a death threat?
jokes.setup: Ron says the office will have to function without her eventually
jokes.punchline: Leslie's paranoid interpretation of career advice
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Leslie's tendency to overthink. Character-appropriate paranoia.

jokes.text: God help me--you've built a well-functioning government agency
jokes.setup: Ron acknowledging Leslie's success
jokes.punchline: Treating government efficiency as personal nightmare
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron character moment - his horror at effective government while showing pride in Leslie. Great contradiction.
